info.title: NextLabs API Reference
info.description:
API Authentication

CloudAz supports OpenID Connect (OIDC)-based authentication for API requests. The following are the general steps to authenticate.

  1. Request an ID token.

    Clients can request an ID token by making the following HTTP POST request to the access token URL: https://<Base URL>/cas/oidc/accessToken.

    The following table lists the request parameters.

    POST request body parameterDescription
    grant_typeThe grant_type value must be password.
    usernameThe CloudAz console user name.
    passwordThe CloudAz password.
    client_idThe value of the registered OIDC service (client). You may use the client_id of the CloudAz OIDC service, which is ControlCenterOIDCClient by default. If you have a custom OIDC service configured, please use the appropriate client_id provided by your OIDC service provider.

    Request headers and body parameter sample

    POST /cas/oidc/accessToken HTTP/1.1
    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
    Accept: */*
    Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
    Connection: keep-alive
    Content-Length: 98

    grant_type=password&username=nextlabsuser&password=strongpassword!&client_id=ControlCenterOIDCClient

    Response headers and body sample

    HTTP/1.1 200 OK
    Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, max-age=0, must-revalidate
    Pragma: no-cache
    Expires: 0
    Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=15768000 ; includeSubDomains
    X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
    X-Frame-Options: DENY
    X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
    WWW-Authenticate: Basic realm="authentication required"
    Content-Type: application/json;charset=UTF-8
    Content-Length: 1797
    Keep-Alive: timeout=60
    Connection: keep-alive

    {
    "access_token": "AT-10-yEZyMs-FL0aodqsKDereRZ5jnhSo0mkr.....",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "expires_in": 1200,
    "refresh_token": "RT-10-6-QO99z9fzLaPOR0yUrHZdW-jk5FFrCa",
    "id_token": "eyJhbGciOiJSU0EtT0FFUC0yNTYiLCJlbmMiOiJBMjU2R0NNIiwia2l...",
    "refresh_token": "RT-3-89AVtKqbSuyAKYx3rM6I2TwyNywSF5J5..",
    "token_type": "bearer",
    "expires_in": 1200,
    "scope": ""
    }

  2. Send the value of the id_token in the Authorization HTTP header. The following is an example raw request of a protected resource using this method.

    Request headers sample

    GET /console/api/v1/policy/mgmt/82 HTTP/1.1
    Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSU0EtT0FFUC0yNTYiLCJlbmMiOiJBMjU2R0NNIiwia2l...
    Accept: */*
    Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br
    Connection: keep-alive

API Workflow guide to create attribute-based policies

  1. Create 3 new tags

    Tags are optionals but efficiently in utilizing for the search related endpoints.

    Create 3 tags with different types. Detailed in [Tag Management] POST/console/api/v1/config/tags/add/{tagType}-Creates a new Tag of the given tag type.

    a. "key": "helpdesk" (POLICY_MODEL_TAG type).

    b. "key": "helpdesk_component" (COMPONENT_TAG type).

    c. "key": "helpdesk_policy" (POLICY_TAG type).

  2. Create a component type (policy model)

    Component types (policy models) represent data attributes which are references utilized within the component.

    Create 1 resource component type (policy model). Detailed in [Component Type Management] POST/console/api/v1/policyModel/mgmt/add-Creates a new component type (policy model)

    a. User (Fixed subject policy model).

    b. Support Tickets (Resource policy model).

  3. Create new components with the component type (policy model).

    Components are used as utilization for the data attributes from Component types (policy models).

    Create 1 subject component, 2 resource components, 3 action components. Detailed in [Component Management] POST/console/api/v1/component/mgmt/add-Creates a new component.

    a. IT Department (Subject component).

    b. Security Vulnerabilities (Resource component).

    c. Tickets in User's Product Area (resource component).

    d. Assign Support Tickets (Action component).

    e. Edit Support Tickets (Action component).

    f. View Support Tickets (Action component).

  4. Create new policies with components

    Policies are used as rules that uses the attributes from the components to decide whether to grant or deny access to the resources.

    Create 3 policies. Detailed in [Policy Management] POST/console/api/v1/policy/mgmt/add-Creates a new policy.

    a. Allow Users in IT to View All Support Tickets (Allow policy).

    b. Allow Users in IT to Edit and Reassign Tickets in their Product Area (Allow policy).

    c. Deny access to Security Vulnerabilities if not Created By or Assigned To the User (Deny policy).

Workflow dependency diagram

A List of attribute-based policies

|

|----> Allow Users in IT to View All Support Tickets (Allow Policy)

|     |----> IT Department (Subject Component)

|     |     |----> User Component Type (Subject Policy Model)

|     |----> View Support Tickets (Action Component)

|           |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

|

|----> Allow Users in IT to Edit and Reassign Tickets in their Product Area (Allow Policy)

|     |----> Tickets in User's Product Area (Resource Component)

|     |     |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

|     |----> Edit Support Tickets (Action Component)

|     |     |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

|     |----> Assign Support Tickets (Action Component)

|           |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

|

|----> Deny access to Security Vulnerabilities if not Created By or Assigned To the User (Deny Policy)

     |----> Security Vulnerabilities (Resource Component)

     |     |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

     |----> View Support Tickets (Action Component)

     |     |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

     |----> Edit Support Tickets (Action Component)

     |     |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

     |----> Assign Support Tickets (Action Component)

           |----> Support Tickets Component Type (Resource Policy Model)

Table of API Operations

No.Operation TagsDescription
1.[Operator Configuration]Retrieving operators configurations related endpoints.
2.[Tag Management]Managing tag configurations related endpoints.
3.[Component Type Management]Managing component type (policy model) related endpoints.
4.[Component Type Search]Searching component type (policy model) related endpoints.
5.[Component Search]Managing component related endpoints.
6.[Component Management]Searching component, managing saved search related endpoints.
7.[Policy Management]Managing policy related endpoints.
8.[Policy Search]Searching policy, managing saved search related endpoints.

Try-it-out feature guide

To use the 'Try it out' feature, please ensure you are authorized by clicking the "Authorize" button below, inputting your access token with prefix of "Bearer" e.g. value= Bearer AT-10-yEZyMs-FL0aodqsKDereRZ5jnhSo0mkr...

Once you've set up the authorization, you may utilize the "Try it out" feature available for each operation.

post.description:
1. A component have mandatory name field.

2. The type field is type of components which are restricted to SUBJECT, RESOURCE, and ACTION.

3. The subject components should be associated with a subject policy model specified in the policyModel field.

4. The resource and action components should be associated to the resource policy model specified in the policyModel field. For more info about resource policy model, please refer to the documentation, [[Component Type Management] POST/console/api/v1/policyModel/mgmt/add-Creates a new component type (policy model)](#/3.%20Component%20Type%20Management/add_PolicyModel).

5. The policyModel field (e.g. Support tickets policy model is resource type for the Security Vulnerabilities component). The policyModel can be retrieved from other endpoint, refer to the documentation, [[Component Type Search] POST/console/api/v1/policyModel/search-Searches for a list of component types based on a given search criteria](#/4.%20Component%20Type%20Search/search_PolicyModel).

6. The tags field are labels that identify or classify objects, which can be refer to the documentation, [[Tag Management]-GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/{type}-retrieve a list of tag based on tag type.](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType)

Execute [[Component Type Search] POST/console/api/v1/policyModel/search] and populate the policy model {id} in the payload accordingly. 3. To utilize the "try it out" feature, please copy the payloads provided and execute them in the designated most bottom payload input field.

post.summary: Deploys a list of components based on the given list of component IDs
post.description:
1. Prior to deployment, ensure that the component IDs you intend to deploy already exists.

2. The component ID must exist before trying to deploy. To create a new component, please refer to the documentation, [[Component Management] POST/console/api/v1/Component/mgmt/add-Creates a new component](#/4.%20Component%20Management/create_Component).

3. Before proceeding with deployment, please validate the dependencies of the components by referring to the [[Component Management] POST/console/api/v1/component/mgmt/findDependencies-Find the dependencies for the components](#/4.%20Component%20Management/find_Component_Dependencies).

4. Any component dependencies listed in the response of /findDependencies should be populated the ids in the deployment request.
5. After a successful deployment, the deploymentTime field is populated and the deployed field is set to true.

post.summary: Searches for a list of components based on a given search criteria
post.description: 1. The search criteria in criteria.fields.field is used to search by group, modelType, status, tags, date, text, empty, hasIncludedIn. The model information can be referred in the documentation ComponentLite. 2. The search by group (criteria.fields.field=group), the allowable values are SUBJECT, RESOURCE, ACTION. e.g. "value": ["RESOURCE"] 3. The search by status (criteria.fields.field=status), the allowable values are DRAFT, APPROVED, OBSOLETE. (APPROVED = DEPLOYED, OBSOLETE = DEACTIVATED). e.g. "value": ["APPROVED"]. 4. The search by type (criteria.fields.field=modelType), the values is based on the policy model name created. e.g. "value": ["Support Tickets"] 5. The search by tag (criteria.fields.field=tags), the allowable values for criteria.fields.nestedField include tags.key, tags.label, tags.type, and tags.status, please refer to documentation, [[Tag Management]-GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/{type}-retrieve a list of tag based on tag type.](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType), e.g. "value": ["helpdesk_component"]. 6. The search by text (criteria.fields.field=text), the text represents a specific term used to search for the name and description of the component effectively. e.g."value": "security" 7. The search by date (criteria.fields.field=lastUpdatedDate, the allowable values for dateOption include PAST_7_DAYS, PAST_30_DAYS, PAST_3_MONTHS, PAST_1_YEAR and CUSTOM (for from date to date). 8. The search by empty (criteria.fields.field=empty), the value is either true (search only for empty component) or false (search all components). 9. The search by hasIncludedIn criteria.fields.field=hasIncludedIn), the value is either true (search including sub components) or false (search excluding sub components).

requestBody.description: SearchCriteriaDTO: A search criteria to search for components.

post.summary: Saves a new component search criteria.
post.description: 1. A new saved component search criteria requires mandatory name, description, and type fields. 2. The type field must be chosen from a predefined set of options, including POLICY, COMPONENT, POLICY_MODEL_RESOURCE, POLICY_MODEL_SUBJECT, LOCATION, and PROPERTY. 3. The search criteria in criteria.fields.field is used to search by group, modelType, status, tags, date, text, empty, hasIncludedIn. The model information can be referred in the documentation ComponentLite. 4. The search by group (criteria.fields.field=group), the allowable values are SUBJECT, RESOURCE, ACTION. e.g. "value": ["RESOURCE"] 5. The search by status (criteria.fields.field=status), the allowable values are DRAFT, APPROVED, OBSOLETE. (APPROVED = DEPLOYED, OBSOLETE = DEACTIVATED). e.g. "value": ["APPROVED"]. 6. The search by type (criteria.fields.field=modelType), the values is based on the policy model name created. e.g. "value": ["Support Tickets"] 7. The search by tag (criteria.fields.field=tags), the allowable values for criteria.fields.nestedField include tags.key, tags.label, tags.type, and tags.status, please refer to documentation, [[Tag Management]-GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/{type}-retrieve a list of tag based on tag type.](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType). e.g. "value": ["helpdesk_component"]. 8. The search by text (criteria.fields.field=text), the text represents a specific term used to search for the name and description of the component effectively. e.g."value": "security" 9. The search by date (criteria.fields.field=lastUpdatedDate, the allowable values for dateOption include PAST_7_DAYS, PAST_30_DAYS, PAST_3_MONTHS, PAST_1_YEAR and CUSTOM (for from date to date). 10. The search by empty (criteria.fields.field=empty), the value is either true (search only for empty component) or false (search all components). 11. The search by hasIncludedIn criteria.fields.field=hasIncludedIn), the value is either true (search including sub components) or false (search excluding sub components).

requestBody.description: SavedSearchDTO: A new search criteria to be saved.

post.description:
1. A policy have mandatory name field.

2. The effectType field is authorization decision for the policy, which can be either "Allow" or "Deny".

3. The tags field are labels that identify or classify objects, please refer to the documentation, [[Tag Management]-GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/{type}-retrieve a list of tag based on tag type.](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType).

4. The subjectComponents field should be populated with subject components e.g. IT department, which can be retrieved from another endpoint, [[Component Search] POST/console/api/v1/component/search/savedlist/{type}](#/6.%20Component%20Search/search_Component), retrieve a list of components based on group type SUBJECT.

5. The fromResourceComponents field should be populated with resource components e.g. Security Vulnerabilities, which can be retrieved from another endpoint, please refer to the documentation, [[Component Search] POST/console/api/v1/component/search/savedlist/{type}](#/6.%20Component%20Search/search_Component), retrieve a list of components based on group type RESOURCE.

6. The actionComponents field should be populated with action components e.g. VIEW,EDIT,ASSIGN Support Tickets, which can be retrieved from another endpoint, please refer to the documentation, [[Component Search] POST/console/api/v1/component/search/savedlist/{type}](#/6.%20Component%20Search/search_Component), retrieve a list of components based on group type ACTION.

requestBody.description: PolicyDTOReq: A new policy (Deny access to Security Vulnerabilities if not Created By or Assigned To the User) to be saved. 1. Execute [[Tag Management]-GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/POLICY_TAG](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType) and populate the tag {id} of helpdesk_policy in the payload accordingly. 2. Execute [[Component Search] POST/console/api/v1/component/search/savedlist/ACTION](#/6.%20Component%20Search/search_Component) and populate the policy model {id} of VIEW,EDIT,ASSIGN Support Tickets in the payload accordingly. 3. Execute [[Component Search] POST/console/api/v1/component/search/savedlist/RESOURCE](#/6.%20Component%20Search/search_Component) and populate the policy model {id} of Security Vulnerabilities in the payload accordingly. 4. To utilize the "try it out" feature, please copy the payloads provided and execute them in the designated most bottom payload input field.

post.summary: Searches for a list of policies based on a given search criteria.
post.description: 1. The search criteria in criteria.fields.field can be used examples such as search by status, tags, effectType, date, text, hasParent. More model information detailed in the PolicyLite. 2. The search by status (criteria.fields.field=status), the allowable values are DRAFT, APPROVED, OBSOLETE. (APPROVED = DEPLOYED, OBSOLETE = DEACTIVATED), e.g."value": "DRAFT". 3. The search by effectType (criteria.fields.field=effectType), the allowable values are authorization decisions, which can be either "Allow" or "Deny" e.g."value": "Allow". 4. The search by tag (criteria.fields.field=tags), the allowable values for criteria.fields.nestedField include tags.key, tags.label, tags.type, and tags.status. This can be refer from documentation, [[Tag Management]-GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/{type}-retrieve a list of tag based on tag type.](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType), e.g. "value": ["helpdesk_policy"]. 5. The search by text (criteria.fields.field=text),the text represents a specific term used to search for the name and description of policy effectively. e.g."value": "Tickets". 6. post.summary: Creates a new component type (policy model)
post.description:
1. A component type (policy model) have mandatory name and short name fields.

2. A subject component types are limited or fixed to USER, HOST, and APPLICATION for subject components.

3. A resource component types can be added/customized for resource or action components. e.g. Support Tickets

4. The tags field are labels that identify or classify objects, which can be refer to the documentation, [[Tag Management] GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/{type}-retrieve a list of tag based on tag type.](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType)

5. The attributes field are associated data, which have mandatory name, data type, and operator(s) fields. The allowable values for the data types are DATE, TIME_INTERVAL, NUMBER, STRING, and MULTIVAL. The Operator(s) field can be refer to the documentation, [[Operator Configuration] GET/console/api/v1/config/dataType/list-retrieve all the operators](#/1.%20Operator%20Configuration/find_All_Operator).

6. The actions field represent operations that can be performed on resource types, such as opening, copying, printing, or sharing. Authorization requests involve accessing resources and performing actions. For e.g. "Assign", "View", "Edit" support tickets. The Action field have mandatory name and short name fields.

7. The obligations field are sets of operations that must be executed by the Policy Enforcement Point (PEP) alongside an authorization decision, which can be either "Allow" or "Deny". The Obligations field have mandatory name and short name fields. Adding parameters to an obligation requires mandatory parameter name and short name.

requestBody.description: PolicyModelDTOReq: A new policy model to be saved. 1. Execute [[Tag Management] GET/console/api/v1/config/tags/list/POLICY_MODEL_TAG](#/2.%20Tag%20Management/find_Tag_ByType) and populate the tag {id} in the payload accordingly. 2. Execute [[Operator Configuration] GET/console/api/v1/config/dataType/list](#/1.%20Operator%20Configuration/find_All_Operator) and populate all the operatorConfigs {id} in the payload accordingly.
